description: Adversarial FR-EN code-switch corpus for TuParles. Each case is a sentence a French-speaking developer would actually dictate, chosen because an English token sits exactly where French phonetics wants to swallow it. `must_contain` = the token that MUST survive (the gate); `must_not_contain` = the plausible misfire it collapses into. The reference `text` doubles as the WER reference. Seeded from real misfires — grow it every time the decoder embarrasses itself.

cases.text: On disait fan out les agents, c'est un bon exemple de parallélisme.
cases.note: REAL MISFIRE 2026-06-23. Dictating 'fan out les agents' produced 'tu fais un air de jamais les agents'. The English particle verb collapses into French 'fais un air'. The bug that started this suite.

cases.text: Je vais spin up trois workers pour absorber la charge.
cases.note: 'spin up' has no French anchor; easily heard as 'speed up' or frenchified.

cases.text: On fait un roll out progressif sur dix pour cent du trafic.
cases.note: 'roll' maps cleanly to French 'roule'; the particle 'out' then drifts to 'tout'.

cases.text: Il faut ship cette feature avant vendredi.
cases.note: The #31 case: ship/chip are a minimal pair Whisper routinely confuses; 'feature' drifts to 'fissure'.

cases.text: Le code est clean, on peut ship it maintenant.
cases.note: 'ship it' run together; also a clean/cline trap.

cases.text: Le cache Redis a expiré, il faut invalider la clé.
cases.note: cache (storage) vs cash (money) vs caché (hidden) — three-way French homophone.

cases.text: Réponds dans le thread Slack, pas en DM.
cases.note: 'thread' (the 'th' is the hardest English phoneme for a French speaker) drifts to trade/tread.

cases.text: Tu peux review ma pull request avant le standup ?
cases.note: Dense switch; 'pull request' is already a known mishear (boule/poule request, see lexicon.py).

cases.text: Le deploy en prod a pété, il faut rollback tout de suite.
cases.note: 'deploy' shatters into 'des ploies'; 'rollback' into 'roule back'.

cases.text: Le meeting de ce matin a duré une heure de trop.
cases.note: 'meeting' lands near French 'mythique'.

cases.text: On doit scale le service pour tenir les five nines.
cases.note: 'scale' (verb borrow) + 'five nines' (English number-as-jargon, not to be digitised).

cases.text: La latency est montée à two hundred milliseconds sur le p99.
cases.note: English number words mid-French; 'latency' drifts to 'la tension' / gets translated to 'latence'.

cases.text: Fais un commit atomique et push sur la branche feature.
cases.note: commit/comité; push/pousse — both translate-or-mishear traps.

cases.text: La CI tourne l'API et le LLM en local sur le GPU.
cases.note: Spoken acronyms in a French frame; each can be spelled out phonetically.

cases.text: Le bug est côté backend, pas dans le frontend React.
cases.note: Compound English nouns split at the seam (back and / front and).

cases.text: On tag la release candidate et on freeze le code.
cases.note: 'release' near 'relâche'; 'freeze' near 'frise'.

cases.text: Bref, je fais le quick fix, je ship en staging, et si c'est green on roll out en prod.
cases.note: Five switches in one sentence — the stress test. 'staging' near 'stagiaire', 'green' near 'graine'.

cases.text: J'ai un merge conflict, je vais rebase sur main.
cases.note: 'conflict' vs French 'conflit'; 'rebase' has no anchor.

cases.text: Tu peux trouver les températures avec des API sur internet.
cases.note: REAL MISFIRE 2026-06-24 (greenfield, zero context). 'des APIs sur internet' decoded as 'des épiailles sur internet'. The English plural acronym (ay-pee-EYEZ) dissolves into French nonsense — the trailing -z of the spoken plural is the trap (/e.pi.aj/ matches the English pronunciation, not French 'a-pé-i'). Reference uses uninflected 'API' (natural FR usage) so the gate isn't brittle on the plural -s. Complements ci-api-llm-gpu (spelled-letter singular).

cases.text: L'idée c'est un highscore qu'on peut partager de manière virale.
cases.note: REAL SURVIVAL 2026-06-24 — pin it so a decode change can't split or translate it. 'highscore' must stay one token (not 'high score') and not be Frenchified to 'score élevé'.

cases.text: On veut un jeu jouable et replayable.
cases.note: REAL SURVIVAL 2026-06-24. The English adjective must not be translated to 'rejouable' nor split into 'replay able'.

cases.text: Fais-moi un artefact interactif self-contained dans Claude.
cases.note: REAL SURVIVAL 2026-06-24. NOTE: the scorer maps hyphen->space, so 'self-contained' and 'self contained' are indistinguishable here; we gate on the tokens surviving and ban the TRANSLATION ('auto-contenu'), not the hyphenation, which this scorer cannot see.
